title: BSP data/DMA/command launch ordering audit
observations: The BSP data path writes REG_BLKSZ and REG_BCNTR before calling sunxi_mmc_start_dma().
The BSP start_dma path initializes descriptors, resets FIFO/DMAIF/DMAC, enables GCTRL DMA, sets IDIE for reads, and enables IDMAC.
The BSP then programs REG_IMASK and REG_CARG, executes wmb(), and writes REG_CMDR.
The local diagnostic branch follows the same ordering: BLKSZ/BCNTR, IDMAC setup/start, IMASK/CARG, wmb(), CMDR.
The local branch also dumps descriptors immediately before CMDR, so the failure evidence is collected after the launch-order barrier.
reason: No BSP command-launch ordering delta is missing locally. The failure remains an IDMAC descriptor-fetch stall after the expected launch sequence.
next: Do not retest command/DMA launch ordering without new evidence for a different A733-specific ordering rule.
